description_en: A classic Ming dynasty hardwood armchair (Sichutou Guanmaoyi) with four protruding ends on the top rail and armrests, featuring an S-curved back splat, splayed legs, and curved stretchers.

components.name: seat_frame
components.description: Rectangular seat frame with central opening for cane or soft mat

components.name: back_left_leg
components.description: Back left leg extending continuously from floor to top rail

components.name: back_right_leg
components.description: Back right leg extending continuously from floor to top rail

components.name: front_left_leg
components.description: Front left leg extending up to armrest height

components.name: front_right_leg
components.description: Front right leg extending up to armrest height

components.name: top_rail
components.description: Characteristic hat-shaped crest rail with ends curving outward and upward
components.position_note: spanning between back leg tops, protruding beyond

components.name: back_splat
components.description: S-curved central back panel between seat and top rail
components.position_note: centered between back legs, above seat frame

components.name: left_armrest
components.description: Curved armrest rail descending from front to back left
components.position_note: left side, protruding forward beyond front leg

components.name: right_armrest
components.description: Curved armrest rail descending from front to back right
components.position_note: right side, protruding forward beyond front leg

components.name: front_apron
components.description: Decorative curved apron under seat front

components.name: left_stretcher
components.description: Side stretcher connecting left front and back legs
components.position_note: left side between legs at 0.18m height

components.name: right_stretcher
components.description: Side stretcher connecting right front and back legs
components.position_note: right side between legs at 0.18m height

style_notes: Ming dynasty hardwood furniture style (明式家具). Features Sichutou (四出头) 'four protruding heads' where the top rail (搭脑) ends curve outward/upward beyond back posts and armrests extend forward beyond front posts. S-curved back splat (曲线靠背板) for ergonomic support. Armrests curve downward in 'goose neck' (鹅脖) profile. Legs splayed outward (侧脚) for stability. Stretchers arranged in 'step by step high' (步步高) progression or level. Joinery uses exposed mortise and tenon (榫卯). Surface decoration may include subtle beading on apron edges or low-relief ruyi motifs on the back splat, but maintains minimalist elegance typical of literati taste.
